01_initial_request.input_data: Recombinant canine IL-31 (rcIL-31) protein, canine IL-31RA (cIL-31RA), and canine OSMRβ (cOSMRβ) receptors
01_initial_request.user_intent: To elucidate the binding pattern and biological activity of canine IL-31 to its receptors to facilitate the development of drugs or antibodies against diseases induced by cIL-31 signaling
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Determine the binding affinity (KD value) of rcIL-31 to cIL-31RA and cOSMRβ

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Canine IL-31 (cIL-31) gene sequence is known. [Gap] Recombinant protein is needed for binding and activity studies. [Decision] To achieve protein production, chose to use E. coli BL21 (DE3) to perform expression of codon-optimized cIL-31 gene inserted into pET21a, expecting to obtain inclusion bodies of rcIL-31.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Recombinant cIL-31 proteins were expressed in the form of inclusion bodies.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] rcIL-31 is expressed as inclusion bodies. [Gap] Protein needs to be refolded into native conformation. [Decision] To achieve functional protein, chose to use Refolding buffer to perform refolding of inclusion bodies, expecting to obtain monomeric refolded rcIL-31.

parameters.composition: 100-mM Tris, 2-mM EDTA, 400-mM l-arginine, 5-mM reduced glutathione, 0.5-mM oxidized glutathione, pH 8.0

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Refolded rcIL-31 protein was concentrated and exchanged with buffer containing 20-mM Tris, 50-mM NaCl, 2% glycerol and pH 8.0.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Refolded protein is in solution. [Gap] Protein needs purification to homogeneity. [Decision] To achieve high purity, chose to use HiLoad 16/600 Superdex 75 PG column to perform size-exclusion chromatography, expecting to obtain purified rcIL-31.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: The refolded proteins were purified by size-exclusion chromatography.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Purified rcIL-31 is available. [Gap] Protein needs labeling for binding assays. [Decision] To achieve detection in FACS/SPR, chose to use EZ-Link NHS-PEG4-Biotin Kit to perform biotinylation of rcIL-31, expecting to obtain biotinylated rcIL-31.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: The purified recombinant cIL-31 (rcIL-31) proteins were biotinylated.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Refolded rcIL-31 is purified. [Gap] Secondary structure needs confirmation to ensure native folding. [Decision] To achieve structural characterization, chose to use Chirascan CD spectropolarimeter to perform CD spectra measurement, expecting to obtain far-UV and near-UV range data.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: The CD spectrum revealed that the refolded rcIL-31 has the characteristic α-helix signature peaks at 192, 208 and 222 nm.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] CD raw data is collected. [Gap] Secondary structure composition needs quantification. [Decision] To achieve structure analysis, chose to use CDNN software to perform secondary structural elements analysis, expecting to obtain percentage of alpha-helices.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: About 96.10% of amino acids of refolded rcIL-31 are folded into α-helices.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Experimental structure data is available. [Gap] Comparison with predicted model needed for validation. [Decision] To achieve structural validation, chose to use AlphaFold server to perform 3D model prediction comparison, expecting to confirm native structure.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: The 3D structure of the refolded rcIL-31 was similar overall to the AlphaFold model which shows mature cIL-31 mainly contains 4 α-helices.

references.relevance: AlphaFold server developed by the DeepMind group.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Receptor genes are available. [Gap] Receptors need to be expressed on cell surface for binding assay. [Decision] To achieve receptor expression, chose to use Linear polyethylenimine (PEI) to perform transient transfection of HEK293T cells, expecting to obtain cells expressing cIL-31RA-EGFP or cOSMRβ-EGFP.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Recombinant plasmid was transiently transfected into HEK293T cells.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Cells express receptors and rcIL-31 is biotinylated. [Gap] Binding efficacy needs measurement. [Decision] To achieve binding quantification, chose to use Beckman MoFlo XDP to perform flow cytometry assay, expecting to obtain binding percentages and EC50 values.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: rcIL-31 binds independently with cIL-31RA and cOSMRβ. EC50 for cIL-31RA was 8.54 µg/ml and for cOSMRβ was 0.81 µg/ml.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] FACS shows binding affinity difference. [Gap] Precise kinetic KD value needed for cOSMRβ. [Decision] To achieve kinetic measurement, chose to use Biacore T200 to perform SPR assay, expecting to obtain KD value.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: rcIL-31 has a high binding affinity to soluble cOSMRβ with a KD value of 35.9 nM (3.59x10^-8 M).

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Binding is confirmed. [Gap] Biological activity (signaling) needs verification. [Decision] To achieve activity validation, chose to use AlphaLISA SureFire Ultra HV kits to perform STAT phosphorylation assay, expecting to obtain pSTAT3/5 levels.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: rcIL-31 could activate both pSTAT3 and pSTAT5 signaling pathways even at the lowest concentration of 0.6 µg/ml.

references.relevance: Confirmed that DH-82 cells express receptors and STAT phosphorylation can be induced.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Binding patterns differ between species. [Gap] Sequence homology needs analysis to explain differences. [Decision] To achieve evolutionary comparison, chose to use Lasergene Megalign program to perform multiple sequence alignment, expecting to obtain identity percentages.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Canine and feline share much higher identities (75.7%, 89.9%, 83.8%) compared to human.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Surface Plasmon Resonance (SPR) and Flow Cytometry (FACS)

KD_cOSMRβ.interpretation: High binding affinity of rcIL-31 to soluble cOSMRβ measured by SPR.

EC50_cOSMRβ.interpretation: Concentration for 50% maximal binding effect to membrane-anchored cOSMRβ.

EC50_cIL-31RA.interpretation: Concentration for 50% maximal binding effect to membrane-anchored cIL-31RA.

Affinity_Ratio.interpretation: Binding affinity of rcIL-31 for cOSMRβ is 10.5 times as high as that for cIL-31RA.

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was achieved. The study successfully determined the binding affinity (KD value of 35.9 nM for cOSMRβ) and elucidated the binding pattern (independent binding, higher affinity to cOSMRβ than cIL-31RA), facilitating drug development against cIL-31 signaling diseases.
